summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering several topics including recognition of the fire/EMS department as Ohio's EMS Department of the Year, a Brownfield grant update on the Combs dumps property, proposed zoning text amendments, and various departmental updates. The board approved payment of bills, zoning text amendments, out-of-town training for a detective, and an authorized credit card users list before adjourning to executive session regarding employee matters.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (March 14, 2023 through March 27, 2023) - passed
Motion to accept proposed zoning text amendments as presented with modifications - passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Detective McMillan for homicide investigations advanced training ($1,375 registration) - passed
Motion to approve authorized credit card users list - passed
Motion to adjourn to executive session under ORC 121.22 G1 - passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:59 PM - passed
action_items: Administrator Rose to follow up with Prosecutor Shrive regarding Combs property access and nuisance declaration status
Administrator Rose to explore gaining access to Brock property (adjacent parcel) for Brownfield assessment
Administrator Rose to send Community Room Policy to prosecuting attorney's office for review on how restrictive usage policies can be
Consultant Fields to finalize zoning text amendments with discussed modifications and submit to prosecuting attorney for review
Consultant Fields to add ORC definition of agricultural building to zoning definitions
Consultant Fields to set up survey monkey for next workshop date
Chief to confirm May 24th date for EMS Department of the Year award ceremony in Columbus
Michael Weinstein (Patriot Engineering) to continue working with Administrator Rose and prosecutor's office to gain property access for Brownfield assessments
key_amounts: Nearly $400,000 in Brownfield assessment grants received for Combs dumps survey
$350 million statewide Ohio program for brownfield assessment and cleanup grants
$6 billion state of Ohio surplus referenced regarding potential additional brownfield funding
Additional $350 million being lobbied for in upcoming fiscal budget for brownfield grants
$1,375 registration cost for Detective McMillan's homicide investigation training
10% potential Medicare reimbursement dock avoided through completed survey
notable_topics: Goshen Fire EMS recognized as Ohio EMS Department of the Year
Goshen Police Department to be recognized as Police Department of the Month in May
Brownfield grant update on Combs dumps - property access challenges with owner
Proposed zoning text amendments including administrative relief, barns/storage sheds in R1, panhandle/flag lots, and accessory structures in front yards
Service department dump truck transmission failure and repair status
O'Banionville bridge replacement April 3 through June 30 - mutual aid arrangements
Station 18 code compliance issues being worked through
EMS continuing education recertification for three years
Homorama coming to Goshen Township in 2024
Tornado recovery updates including park shelter and Teasdale Fenton wall repairs
Authorized credit card users list approval
Lions Club district meeting - Goshen recognized as most giving and productive
New employee Mr. Helton started in service department
notes: The meeting had an unusually positive and celebratory tone with multiple state-level recognitions for both fire/EMS and police departments. The Combs dumps Brownfield situation has been ongoing for approximately 14 years with the property owner resisting access. Brownfield grant extensions were secured until June 30, 2024. Executive session lasted approximately 2 hours (6:56 PM to 8:58 PM) regarding employee appointment/employment/compensation. The roll call order was noted as having changed, with staff working on perfecting the process.
